I agree with the conclusion provided earlier. One additional point supporting the involvement of the obturator nerve is that it innervates muscles like the gracilis and pectineus which can become painful upon compression or irritation during the McRoberts maneuver. This further reinforces the idea that symptoms related to these muscle groups often correlate with obturator nerve compromise rather than just general peripheral neuropathy effects seen in conditions affecting multiple nerves simultaneously.
